parts.text:
Now I need to update the Auth.css file with the new styling. Let me apply all the changes:

1. LAYOUT - update .auth-left
2. HEADLINE - update .auth-left h2, .auth-left .auth-headline
3. SUBTEXT - update .auth-left .auth-subtext
4. FEATURE PILLS - update .feature-item and .feature-list
5. ADD mock-visual styles
6. COPYRIGHT - update .auth-left .auth-copyright

Let me do this with multiple edits.
Now let me update the headline styles.
Now I need to add the mock-visual styles. Let me add them after the feature-item styles.
Now I need to update the copyright styles.
Now I need to add the mock-visual div to Login.tsx and Register.tsx. Let me find these files first.
Now I need to read both Login.tsx and Register.tsx to find where to add the mock-visual div.

parts.text:
Wait, the user specifically said "Fix the left panel (.auth-left) styling in Auth.css only. Do not touch any TSX logic." But then they asked me to add the mock-visual div to Login.tsx and Register.tsx. Let me re-read the request.

Actually, the user asked me to:
1. Fix styling in Auth.css only (done)
2. Add a mock visual card at the bottom of .auth-left - Add a new div .mock-visual in Login.tsx and Register.tsx

The second part explicitly asks me to add the mock-visual to the TSX files. So I did the right thing. The "Do not touch any TSX logic" probably means don't change the form logic, state management, or anything functional - just add the HTML markup.

Let me verify that the changes were applied correctly by reading the updated files.
